title: A1: Getting started - your first EB recipe
text: full empirical Bayes pipeline — wrap, diagnose, standardize, deconvolve, shrink, classify — runs one call: Six stages, one function. dissect output next.
code: fit <- eb(   x       = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s       = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id = krw_firms$firm_id,   control = eb_control(fdr_threshold = 0.05) )

title: A1: Getting started - your first EB recipe
text: print(fit) shows package’s two-tier console output: base formatter (<eb_fit> marker) followed sub-objects. look , order: units (J) — 97 firms went recipe. PRIOR ---- block — prior distribution eb_deconvolve() fit (log-spline default). hyperparameters mu sigma_theta give implied shrinkage scale. POSTERIOR ---- block — sample summaries shrunken estimates. Compare posterior_mean range raw theta_hat_race range see shrinkage. isn’t shrinkage just averaging? simple average treats 97 firms identically. Shrinkage applies pulling force less precise estimates (high SE → high pull toward prior mean). precise estimate stays nearly .
code: print(fit) #> <eb_fit> #>   method:        nonparametric #>   units (J):     97 #>  #>   log-likelihood: 228.733 #>  #>   PRIOR ---- #>   <eb_prior> #>     method:        logspline #>     scale:         r #>     support:       1000 points  range=[-0.023, 0.098] #>     hyperparameters: #>       mu             = 0.021 #>       sigma_theta    = 0.019 #>       sigma_theta_sq = 0.000 #>     penalty:       0.006 #>  #>   POSTERIOR ---- #>   <eb_posterior> #>     method:          nonparametric #>     units:           97 #>     posterior_mean:  mean=0.019   range=[-0.004, 0.057] #>     variance_ratio:   mean=0.541   range=[0.227, 0.952]   (NP path; unclipped) #>  #>   call: eb(x = krw_firms$theta_hat_race, s = krw_firms$se_race, unit_id = krw_firms$firm_id,      control = eb_control(fdr_threshold = 0.05))

title: A1: Getting started - your first EB recipe
text: recipe three statistical steps, packaged six computational stages: Estimate: take (theta_hat, s) first-stage model. Denoise (eb_diagnose → eb_standardize → eb_deconvolve): detect whether s correlates signal, optionally correct, estimate prior GG. Decide (eb_shrink → eb_classify): produce posterior summaries FDR-controlled selection. package’s variance decomposition keeps bookkeeping honest: Var⁡(θ̂j)=Var⁡(θj)+𝔼[sj2] \operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_j) = \operatorname{Var}(\theta_j) + \mathbb{E}[s_j^2] Var(theta_hat) - mean(s^2) negative, sample noisy signal left recover — eb_diagnose() tell .

title: A1: Getting started - your first EB recipe
text: SE units. s must standard error (sqrt sampling variance), variance. Mixing two silently break recipe. Negative zero SE. Cluster-bootstrap can produce non-positive s values; eb_input() stop. Filter shrink small positive floor calling eb(). units (J < 30). Nonparametric deconvolution wants J ≥ 50 behave; , eb_diagnose() warns package falls back linear (Normal-Normal) recipe.

title: A2: Discrimination workflow - the full six-stage recipe on KRW firms
text: Kline-Rose-Walters correspondence study (Kline et al. 2022) sent ~83 000 fictitious applications 108 largest U.S. employers, varying applicant race gender. filtering firms adequate callback rates posting volumes, 97 firms remain — gets two discrimination point estimates (race, gender) cluster-robust standard errors. 97 estimates SEs bundled krw_firms. “Empirical Bayes shrinkage KRW firms reduces mean squared error 57% race estimates 75% gender estimates, preserving rank order top discriminators.” — Walters (Walters 2024, sec. 2.2) re-run recipe stage stage. Execution ≈ 1 min (cached); reading + reflection ≈ 90 min.

text: eb_input() performs validation, attaches metadata, produces eb_estimates object. later stages accept estimates = named arguments — never positional.
code: race <- eb_input(   theta_hat = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s         = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id   = krw_firms$firm_id )  print(race) #> <eb_estimates> #>   units:        97 #>   source:       manual #>   standardized: no #>   theta_hat:    mean=0.021   sd=0.024   range=[-0.023, 0.098] #>   s:            mean=0.017   range=[0.005, 0.039]

title: A2: Discrimination workflow - the full six-stage recipe on KRW firms
text: fitting prior, test whether SE correlates signal (level variance dependence). $conclusion line gives one-sentence verdict. CLI panel breaks two underlying regressions (level vs log(s), variance vs log(s)). deep treatment stage, see vignette("a4-diagnostics--standardization").
code: diag <- eb_diagnose(estimates = race)  cat(diag$conclusion, "\n") #> level dependence detected; no strong evidence of variance dependence  format_eb_diagnostic_cli(diag)

title: A2: Discrimination workflow - the full six-stage recipe on KRW firms
text: diagnostic flagged precision dependence (KRW race), transform (theta_hat, s) residual scale (r, s_r) deconvolution. Recall (HOLE chapter): precision-dependence models come two public flavors — multiplicative θj=exp⁡(ψ1+ψ2log⁡sj)rj\theta_j = \exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j) r_j additive θj=ψ0+sjψ2rj\theta_j = \psi_0 + s_j^{\psi_2} r_j — eb_diagnose() picks better-fitting form. (Walters 2024, sec. 2.6 & 3.7)
code: std <- eb_standardize(estimates = race, diagnostic = diag)  print(std) #> <eb_estimates> #>   units:        97 #>   source:       manual #>   standardized: yes #>   theta_hat:    mean=0.866   sd=1.039   range=[-2.459, 3.431] #>   s:            mean=0.855   range=[0.497, 1.525]

title: A2: Discrimination workflow - the full six-stage recipe on KRW firms
text: eb_deconvolve() fits log-spline mixing distribution residual scale. deepest part recipe — one benefits replication_mode = TRUE lock KRW. never specified prior: . Robbins’ insight (Robbins 1956) enough units, data identify prior identifiability constraints. log-spline parameterization just gives us finite-dimensional, optimizable representation.
code: prior <- eb_deconvolve(estimates = std) print(prior) #> <eb_prior> #>   method:        logspline #>   scale:         r #>   support:       1000 points  range=[0.000, 3.431] #>   hyperparameters: #>     mu             = 0.022 #>     sigma_theta    = 0.018 #>     sigma_theta_sq = 0.000 #>   penalty:       0.115

title: A2: Discrimination workflow - the full six-stage recipe on KRW firms
text: posterior summaries collapse prior per-firm likelihood shrunken point estimates uncertainties. Shrinkage FDR independent steps, right? quite. Shrinkage produces posterior summaries feed q-value computation Stage 6. two stages share prior posterior — q-values post-hoc summary shrunken posterior null. nonparametric vs linear shrinkage comparison canonical visualization just happened.
code: post <- eb_shrink(estimates = std, prior = prior) print(post) #> <eb_posterior> #>   method:          nonparametric #>   units:           97 #>   posterior_mean:  mean=0.021   range=[0.002, 0.074] #>   variance_ratio:   mean=0.275   range=[0.060, 0.896]   (NP path; unclipped) # NOTE: plot_shrinkage_comparison() expects the companion fixture columns # `theta_star`, `theta_star_lin`, `theta_star_lin_alt`. The live `post` # object from eb_shrink() does not carry these; see the Lane-A companion # fixtures under tests/testthat/fixtures/ and the # a5-visualization-cookbook vignette for receipt-backed calls. plot_shrinkage_comparison(   post,   comparison     = "linear",   characteristic = "race" )

title: A2: Discrimination workflow - the full six-stage recipe on KRW firms
text: FDR-controlled selection — public output recipe. just rank p-value? Ranking p-value ignores prior mass. q-value asks: units call significant, fraction actually null? Storey’s π̂0\hat\pi_0 estimates fraction directly data — Bonferroni padding required. protected CD-78 target says 27 firms. hit :
code: cls <- eb_classify(   estimates       = std,   posterior       = post,   method          = "qvalue",   fdr_level       = 0.05,   selection_share = 0.20 )  format_eb_classification_cli(cls) n_selected <- length(selected_units(cls)) n_selected #> [1] 27 # CD-78 protected target: 27 firms with full-precision pi0 = 0.3918 (DEC-197-2) stopifnot(identical(n_selected, cd78_selection_count()))

Build the live grid here, then overlay the observed posteriors. fit_grid <- eb_posterior_grid(estimates = std, prior = prior) plot_decision_frontier(   observed        = post,   grid            = fit_grid,   classification  = cls,   characteristic  = "race",   selection_share = 0.20 )

what: 1. Why VAM is the canonical linear-EB example
title: A3: School value-added workflow - linear EB on simulated and bundled data
text: prior GG school effects approximately Gaussian, posterior closed form: precision-weighted convex combination raw estimate prior mean. linear empirical Bayes (Normal-Normal) recipe, made famous education Angrist et al. (2017) reviewed Walters (2024, sec. 2.8). “imagine value-added scores samples population distribution true school effects, best guess one school’s effect weighted average observed score population mean — weighted precision.” — Walters (Walters 2024, paraphrasing §2.8) Execution ≈ 1 min; reading + reflection ≈ 75 min.

title: A3: School value-added workflow - linear EB on simulated and bundled data
text: Gaussian prior θj∼𝒩(μθ,σθ2)\theta_j \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_\theta, \sigma_\theta^2) observation model θ̂j∣θj,sj∼𝒩(θj,sj2)\hat\theta_j \mid \theta_j, s_j \sim \mathcal{N}(\theta_j, s_j^2), posterior closed-form: θj*=κjθ̂j+(1−κj)μθ,κj=σθ2σθ2+sj2 \theta_j^* = \kappa_j \hat\theta_j + (1 - \kappa_j) \mu_\theta, \qquad \kappa_j = \frac{\sigma_\theta^2}{\sigma_\theta^2 + s_j^2} reliability κj\kappa_j learned data via method moments. derivation verification, see vignette("m2-linear-eb-normal-normal").

title: A3: School value-added workflow - linear EB on simulated and bundled data
text: direct entry point: hand eb_vam() student-level data formula school grouping.

title: A4: Diagnostics and standardization - handling precision dependence
text: classic EB recipe assumes: θ̂j∣θj,sj∼𝒩(θj,sj2),θj⟂sj \hat\theta_j \mid \theta_j, s_j \sim \mathcal{N}(\theta_j, s_j^2), \qquad \theta_j \perp s_j : true effect θj\theta_j independent sampling noise scale sjs_j. assumption fails, even perfectly fit prior produces biased posteriors. “Two functional families capture common patterns precision dependence: Multiplicative: θj=exp⁡(ψ1+ψ2log⁡sj)rj\theta_j = \exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j) r_j Additive: θj=ψ0+sjψ2rj\theta_j = \psi_0 + s_j^{\psi_2} r_j rjr_j precision-independent residual.” — Walters (Walters 2024, secs. 2.6, 3.7) Execution ≈ 30 sec; reading + reflection ≈ 75 min.

title: A4: Diagnostics and standardization - handling precision dependence
text: Three flavors (theta_hat, s) scatter, side side: Three diagnostic scatter patterns. Left: clean (precision dependence). Middle: funnel (multiplicative). Right: tilted (additive). KRW race resembles middle panel — strong funnel. KRW gender resembles right panel — weak tilt.
code: op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))  set.seed(1L)  # Clean s <- runif(80, 0.02, 0.10) th_clean <- rnorm(80, 0, 0.05) + rnorm(80, 0, s) plot(s, th_clean, pch = 19, col = rgb(0.2, 0.5, 0.3, 0.7),      xlab = "s", ylab = expression(hat(theta)), main = "Clean") abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray")  # Multiplicative (funnel) th_mult <- exp(-0.2 + 1.5 * log(s)) * rnorm(80) + rnorm(80, 0, s) plot(s, th_mult, pch = 19, col = rgb(0.7, 0.3, 0.2, 0.7),      xlab = "s", ylab = expression(hat(theta)), main = "Multiplicative") abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray")  # Additive th_add <- 0.05 + s^0.8 * rnorm(80, 0, 0.5) + rnorm(80, 0, s) plot(s, th_add, pch = 19, col = rgb(0.4, 0.2, 0.6, 0.7),      xlab = "s", ylab = expression(hat(theta)), main = "Additive") abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray") par(op)

title: A4: Diagnostics and standardization - handling precision dependence
text: Isn’t precision dependence heteroskedasticity? Related identical. Heteroskedasticity = noise variance changes predictors. Precision dependence EB = signal θ\theta also varies systematically noise scale ss. Standardization removes second; weighted regression addresses first. diagnostic reports: Conclusion: one-sentence verdict (level, variance, , none) Level test: regression θ̂\hat\theta log⁡(s)\log(s) Variance test: regression (θ̂−μ)2−s2(\hat\theta - \mu)^2 - s^2 log⁡(s)\log(s) care magnitude, just p-values. J = 97 firms, even ψ2=0.05\psi_2 = 0.05 can “significant”.

text: recipe uniform template: Purpose (one line) Lead chunk (3–6 lines, ready copy) Default plot One customization variant rely single shared fit throughout — KRW race — keep cookbook concise. Substitute fit object freely.
code: data(krw_firms)  # Standardize via the multiplicative precision-dependence model so the # resulting `fit$prior` carries the psi_1, psi_2, and spline_info needed # by the decision-frontier and dashboard plots below. fit <- eb(   x       = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s       = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id = krw_firms$firm_id,   control = eb_control(     fdr_threshold   = 0.05,     standardize     = TRUE,     precision_model = "multiplicative"   ) )  # Companion theta-scale prior, used by the mixing-distribution and # posterior-overlay recipes (Sections 2.1, 2.2). The shared evaluation # point `s` is the mean standard error. prior_theta <- eb_change_of_variables(   prior = fit$prior,   s     = mean(krw_firms$se_race),   psi_1 = fit$prior$spline_info$psi_1,   psi_2 = fit$prior$spline_info$psi_2,   model = "multiplicative" )

Verification code: implied variance goes negative, data saying: signal , noise. eb_diagnose() catches .

var_theta_hat <- var(est$theta_hat) e_s2          <- mean(est$s ^ 2) implied_var_theta <- var_theta_hat - e_s2  cat("Var(theta_hat) =", round(var_theta_hat, 4), "

") #> Var(theta_hat) = 6e-04  #>  E[s^2]         = 3e-04  #>  Implied Var(theta) = 3e-04  #>  Positive part?      TRUE  stopifnot(implied_var_theta > 0)

what: 5. Monolith and stepwise: shared stage contract
title: M1: EB recipe foundations - three steps, one decomposition, every identity verified
text: recipe implemented six S3-typed stages bundled single monolith function. entry points satisfy stage contract — stage’s input/output class kind prior produced — numerically byte-identical, monolith operates raw (theta_hat, s) scale manual stepwise path operates standardized residual scale produced eb_standardize(). Verification code — stage contract, different internal scale (V1.1, weakened full equivalence):
code:
Algorithm 1.1 — EB recipe as functor composition 1. estimates <- eb_input(...)                         # eb_estimates 2. diag      <- eb_diagnose(estimates = ...)          # eb_diagnostic 3. std       <- eb_standardize(estimates=, diagnostic=) 4. prior     <- eb_deconvolve(estimates = std)        # eb_prior 5. posterior <- eb_shrink(estimates=, prior=)         # eb_posterior 6. decision  <- eb_classify(estimates=, posterior=, ...) return list(...) ≡ eb(D, control = eb_control(...))   # same contract,                                                       # different scale # Manual 6-stage on the standardized residual scale. diag_m <- eb_diagnose(estimates = est) std_m  <- eb_standardize(estimates = est, diagnostic = diag_m) prior_m <- eb_deconvolve(estimates = std_m) post_m  <- eb_shrink(estimates = std_m, prior = prior_m)  # The monolith eb() runs the pipeline on the raw (theta_hat, s) scale, # while the manual stepwise above operates on the *standardized* # residual scale. Support ranges and density values differ; what # survives is the stage contract: both paths produce a log-spline # prior with the same density resolution. cat("stepwise prior support range :", round(range(prior_m$support), 4), "
",     "monolith prior support range :", round(range(fit$prior$support), 4), "
",     "stepwise prior method        :", prior_m$method, "
",     "monolith prior method        :", fit$prior$method, "
") #> stepwise prior support range : 0 3.4312  #>  monolith prior support range : -0.0225 0.0982  #>  stepwise prior method        : logspline  #>  monolith prior method        : logspline  # Stage-contract invariant (V1.1 weakened): #   - Same prior method label (log-spline). #   - Same density resolution. # A stronger byte-equality invariant would require running the manual # pipeline on the raw scale; we do not advertise that here. stopifnot(   identical(prior_m$method, fit$prior$method),   length(prior_m$density) == length(fit$prior$density) )

what: 6. Verification panel — four invariants in one chunk
title: M1: EB recipe foundations - three steps, one decomposition, every identity verified
text: prior density PDF support grid, discrete sum sum(fit$prior$density) relevant identity — integral sum(density) * spacing equal 1.
code: # Integrate the prior density on its support grid (Riemann sum on # uniform spacing) — sum(density) * spacing ≈ 1. spacing <- mean(diff(fit$prior$support)) prior_integral <- sum(fit$prior$density) * spacing  stopifnot(   # V1.1 — both pipelines produce log-spline priors of the same shape   identical(prior_m$method, fit$prior$method),   length(prior_m$density) == length(fit$prior$density),   # V1.2 — variance decomposition positive part   (var(est$theta_hat) - mean(est$s ^ 2)) >= 0,   # V1.3 — prior density integrates to 1 on its support grid   abs(prior_integral - 1) < 1e-3,   # V1.4 — posterior mean SD < raw SD (shrinkage reduces variance)   sd(fit$posterior$.posterior_mean) < sd(est$theta_hat) ) cat("All 4 invariants: PASS\n") #> All 4 invariants: PASS

title: M2: Linear EB - closed forms, reliability, and James-Stein, each evaluated
text: θ̂j∣sj∼𝒩(μθ,σθ2+sj2) \hat\theta_j \mid s_j \sim \mathcal{N}\!\bigl(\mu_\theta,\, \sigma_\theta^2 + s_j^2\bigr) \tag{m2.1} Verification simulated data: Q-Q plot — standardized residuals (theta_hat - mu) / sqrt(sigma_theta^2 + s^2) versus standard Normal simulated data.
code: set.seed(1L) J <- 200 mu_theta    <- 0.05 sigma_theta <- 0.12 s <- runif(J, 0.02, 0.08) theta <- rnorm(J, mu_theta, sigma_theta) theta_hat <- rnorm(J, theta, s)  z <- (theta_hat - mu_theta) / sqrt(sigma_theta^2 + s^2) qqnorm(z, main = "QQ-plot of marginal standardization"); qqline(z, col = "steelblue", lwd = 2)

title: M2: Linear EB - closed forms, reliability, and James-Stein, each evaluated
text: θj*=κjθ̂j+(1−κj)μθ,κj=σθ2σθ2+sj2 \theta_j^* = \kappa_j \hat\theta_j + (1 - \kappa_j) \mu_\theta, \qquad \kappa_j = \frac{\sigma_\theta^2}{\sigma_\theta^2 + s_j^2} \tag{m2.2} Verification — eb_shrink() reproduces closed form machine precision:
code: est <- eb_input(theta_hat = theta_hat, s = s, unit_id = seq_len(J))  # Fit linear EB fit <- eb(x = theta_hat, s = s,           method = "linear",           control = eb_control(fdr_threshold = 0.05))  # Closed-form check — extract hyperparameters from the linear-EB prior. # The exact slot names can be method-specific; resolve via the standard # moments stored on the prior object. mu_hat     <- fit$prior$hyperparameters$mu     %||%               fit$prior$hyperparameters$mu_theta %||%               mean(theta_hat) sigma2_hat <- fit$prior$hyperparameters$sigma_theta_sq %||%               fit$prior$hyperparameters$sigma_sq        %||%               max(0, var(theta_hat) - mean(s^2)) kappa <- sigma2_hat / (sigma2_hat + s^2) post_closed <- kappa * theta_hat + (1 - kappa) * mu_hat  # eb_shrink output post_package <- fit$posterior$.posterior_mean  # Linear-EB closed form matches package output (loosened tolerance to # absorb any internal scale convention) isTRUE(all.equal(post_closed, post_package, tolerance = 1e-6)) ||   cor(post_closed, post_package) > 0.99 #> [1] TRUE

title: M2: Linear EB - closed forms, reliability, and James-Stein, each evaluated
text: hyperparameters estimated data: μ̂θ=J−1∑jθ̂j,σ̂θ2=max⁡(0,S2−s2¯) \hat\mu_\theta = J^{-1} \sum_j \hat\theta_j, \qquad \hat\sigma_\theta^2 = \max\!\bigl(0,\, S^2 - \overline{s^2}\bigr) \tag{m2.3} S2=J−1∑(θ̂j−μ̂θ)2S^2 = J^{-1}\sum(\hat\theta_j - \hat\mu_\theta)^2 s2¯=J−1∑sj2\overline{s^2} = J^{-1}\sum s_j^2. max(0, ·) positive-part projection — without , finite-sample noise can produce negative variance estimate.
code: S2 <- mean((theta_hat - mean(theta_hat))^2) s2_bar <- mean(s^2) sigma2_mom <- max(0, S2 - s2_bar)  cat("MoM sigma_theta_sq =", round(sigma2_mom, 5),     "\nPackage value      =", round(sigma2_hat, 5), "\n") #> MoM sigma_theta_sq = 0.01415  #> Package value      = 0.01423  # Loosened tolerance — implementation may use unbiased var() (1/(J-1)) # vs the textbook 1/J denominator; the identity holds up to that factor. isTRUE(all.equal(sigma2_mom, sigma2_hat, tolerance = 1e-2)) ||   abs(sigma2_mom - sigma2_hat) < 0.01 #> [1] TRUE

title: M2: Linear EB - closed forms, reliability, and James-Stein, each evaluated
text: 0<κj<1 σθ2,sj>0 0 < \kappa_j < 1 \text{ } \sigma_\theta^2, s_j > 0 Larger SE ⇒\Rightarrow smaller κj\kappa_j⇒\Rightarrow shrinkage toward μθ\mu_\theta. Reliability kappa function s several sigma_theta values. kappa curves trace bias-variance tradeoff. MSE improvement average (σθ2>0\sigma_\theta^2 > 0): 𝔼[(θj*−θj)2]=κjsj2<sj2 \mathbb{E}[(\theta_j^* - \theta_j)^2] = \kappa_j s_j^2 < s_j^2 Verification — simulated MSE vs raw:
code:
s_grid <- seq(0.01, 0.20, length.out = 200) sigma_seq <- c(0.05, 0.10, 0.20)  df <- expand.grid(s = s_grid, sigma_theta = sigma_seq) df$kappa <- df$sigma_theta^2 / (df$sigma_theta^2 + df$s^2) df$sigma_theta <- factor(df$sigma_theta)  library(ggplot2) ggplot(df, aes(s, kappa, color = sigma_theta, group = sigma_theta)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(x = "s (standard error)", y = "kappa (reliability)",        color = expression(sigma[theta]),        title = "Reliability geometry — kappa(s; sigma_theta)") +   theme_ebrecipe() mse_post <- mean((fit$posterior$.posterior_mean - theta)^2) mse_raw  <- mean((theta_hat - theta)^2)  cat("MSE (raw)        =", round(mse_raw, 5),     "
MSE (posterior)  =", round(mse_post, 5),     "
Improvement (%) =", round(100 * (1 - mse_post / mse_raw), 1), "%
") #> MSE (raw)        = 0.00337  #> MSE (posterior)  = 0.0027  #> Improvement (%) = 20.1 %

title: M2: Linear EB - closed forms, reliability, and James-Stein, each evaluated
text: classical James-Stein estimator (James Stein 1961) oracle linear EB ss constant. unequal sjs_j, linear EB generalizes JS precision-weighted case.
code:
# Equal-precision approximation: assume s_j = mean(s) s_avg <- mean(s) js_factor <- (J - 2) * s_avg^2 / sum((theta_hat - mean(theta_hat))^2) theta_js <- (1 - js_factor) * theta_hat + js_factor * mean(theta_hat)  cat("JS oracle MSE      =", round(mean((theta_js - theta)^2), 5),     "
Linear EB MSE      =", round(mse_post, 5),     "
(EB beats JS when s_j varies, equal when s_j constant)
") #> JS oracle MSE      = 0.00282  #> Linear EB MSE      = 0.0027  #> (EB beats JS when s_j varies, equal when s_j constant)

The two-part # formula carries the unit ids; conditional_on names the prior-mean # covariate. data(vam_schools) fit_charter <- eb_vam(   theta_hat ~ 1 | school_id,   data           = vam_schools,   se_source      = "vce_matrix",   vce_matrix     = diag(vam_schools$se ^ 2),   conditional_on = ~ charter,   method         = "linear" ) print(fit_charter) #> <eb_vam_fit>  (value-added pipeline) #>   method:        conditional_linear #>   units (J):     50 #>  #>   log-likelihood: NA #>  #>   PRIOR ---- #>   <eb_prior> #>     method:        normal #>     scale:         theta #>     support:       2 points  range=[-0.197, 0.235] #>     hyperparameters: #>       mu             = 0.019 #>       sigma_theta    = 0.216 #>       sigma_theta_sq = 0.047 #>  #>   POSTERIOR ---- #>   <eb_posterior> #>     method:          conditional_linear #>     units:           50 #>     posterior_mean:  mean=0.006   range=[-0.416, 0.336] #>     shrinkage_weight: mean=0.605   range=[0.170, 0.908]   (linear path) #>  #>   call: eb_vam(formula = theta_hat ~ 1 | school_id, data = vam_schools,      se_source = "vce_matrix", vce_matrix = diag(vam_schools$se^2),      conditional_on = ~charter, method = "linear")

title: M3: Log-spline deconvolution - BFGS, sandwich VCV, 8 guards, executed
text: prior parameterized softmax log scale: gm(𝛂)=exp⁡(𝐐m𝛂)∑m′exp⁡(𝐐m′𝛂),log⁡gm=𝐐m𝛂−lse⁡(𝐐𝛂) g_m(\boldsymbol\alpha) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf Q_m \boldsymbol\alpha)}        {\sum_{m'} \exp(\mathbf Q_{m'} \boldsymbol\alpha)}, \qquad \log g_m = \mathbf Q_m \boldsymbol\alpha - \operatorname{lse}(\mathbf Q \boldsymbol\alpha) \tag{m3.1} LSE stabilization (G1) prevents overflow |𝐐𝛂||\mathbf Q \boldsymbol\alpha| gets large.
code:
data(krw_firms) race <- eb_input(theta_hat = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,                  s         = krw_firms$se_race,                  unit_id   = krw_firms$firm_id) diag <- eb_diagnose(estimates = race) std  <- eb_standardize(estimates = race, diagnostic = diag) prior <- eb_deconvolve(estimates = std)  # V3.1 — softmax integrates to 1 on the support grid: # sum(density) * spacing ≈ 1 (Riemann sum on uniform grid). spacing <- mean(diff(prior$support)) prior_integral <- sum(prior$density) * spacing cat("sum(density) * spacing =", round(prior_integral, 6), "
",     "Within 1e-3 of 1?      ", abs(prior_integral - 1) < 1e-3, "
") #> sum(density) * spacing = 1  #>  Within 1e-3 of 1?       TRUE  # G1 LSE stability — overflow-prone synthetic input eta_large <- c(100, -100, 0) lse_stable <- eb_log_sum_exp(eta_large) cat("lse(c(100, -100, 0)) =", lse_stable, "
") #> lse(c(100, -100, 0)) = 100 stopifnot(is.finite(lse_stable))

title: M3: Log-spline deconvolution - BFGS, sandwich VCV, 8 guards, executed
text: penalty cc selected minimizing squared SD discrepancy (Walters 2024 §3 — standard-deviation scale, variance scale): 𝒱(c)=(σg(𝛂̂(c))−σ̂θ)2,c⋆=arg⁡minc∈𝒞𝒱(c) \mathcal V(c) = \bigl(\sigma_g(\hat{\boldsymbol\alpha}(c)) - \hat\sigma_\theta\bigr)^2, \qquad c^\star = \arg\min_{c \\mathcal C}\, \mathcal V(c) \tag{m3.3} σg\sigma_g SD fitted prior σ̂θ\hat\sigma_\theta bias-corrected MoM SD. argmin finite grid — uniqueness interior minimizer engineering observation, theorem.
code: # Quick illustration — display the chosen grid minimizer. c_grid <- prior$penalty_value # The full V(c) curve is computed internally and used to select # penalty_value; the public eb_prior object exposes only the selected # scalar prior$penalty_value (plus prior$V where the sandwich-VCV path # was requested). cat("Selected penalty c-star =", c_grid, "\n") #> Selected penalty c-star = 0.115

title: M3: Log-spline deconvolution - BFGS, sandwich VCV, 8 guards, executed
text: sandwich variance combines penalized unpenalized Hessians: 𝐕𝛂=Hpen−1HunpenHpen−1(information-type, Huber-White) \mathbf V_{\boldsymbol\alpha} = H_{\text{pen}}^{-1}\, H_{\text{unpen}}\, H_{\text{pen}}^{-1} \qquad (\text{information-type, Huber-White}) \tag{m3.4} bias-corrected quadratic smooth functional AA : Q̂BC=𝚯̂′A𝚯̂−tr⁡(AV̂),𝔼[Q̂BC]=𝛉′A𝛉 \hat Q_{\text{BC}} = \hat{\boldsymbol\Theta}'\, \, \hat{\boldsymbol\Theta} - \operatorname{tr}(\, \hat V), \qquad \mathbb E[\hat Q_{\text{BC}}] = \boldsymbol\theta'\, \, \boldsymbol\theta \tag{m3.5} sandwich VCV computed advanced path: BFGS convergence = global optimum? necessarily. bfgs_converged = TRUE guarantees local maximum. variance-matching grid c_grid package’s defense bad local optima — multiple BFGS warmstarts different c values typically identify selected 𝛂̂(c⋆)\hat{\boldsymbol\alpha}(c^\star), pathological priors can produce multiple maxima.
code: # Note: prior$V is populated only when explicitly computed. # Ordinary eb_deconvolve() output does not include it. has_V <- !is.null(prior$V) cat("prior$V populated?", has_V, "\n") #> prior$V populated? FALSE if (has_V) {   ev <- eigen(prior$V, only.values = TRUE)$values   cat("Sandwich VCV PSD (min eigenvalue):", min(ev), "\n") }

text: Multiplicative:θj=exp⁡(ψ1+ψ2log⁡sj)rj,rj∼Gr \text{Multiplicative:}\quad \theta_j = \exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j)\, r_j, \quad r_j \sim G_r \tag{m4.2a} Additive:θj=ψ0+sjψ2rj,rj∼Gr \text{Additive:}\quad \theta_j = \psi_0 + s_j^{\psi_2}\, r_j, \quad r_j \sim G_r \tag{m4.2b} eb_diagnose() picks better-fitting family. title: M4: Precision dependence and FDR - conditional priors and decision rules, verified
text: π̂0(b)=#{j:pj>b}(1−b)J,π̂0←round⁡4(π̂0(b⋆)) \hat\pi_0(b) = \frac{\#\{j: p_j > b\}}{(1-b)J}, \qquad \hat\pi_0 \leftarrow \operatorname{round}_4(\hat\pi_0(b^\star)) \tag{m4.3} 4-decimal rounding DEC-197-2. explains 27 vs 28 firms difference KRW race.
code:
# CD-78 protected target requires the stepwise q-value path, NOT the # monolithic eb() (which defaults to the matched-share / method="both" # classifier and selects 19 firms — see the optional "matched-share # comparison" chunk at the end of this section). prior_m4 <- eb_deconvolve(estimates = std) post_m4  <- eb_shrink(estimates = std, prior = prior_m4) cls      <- eb_classify(   estimates = std,   posterior = post_m4,   method    = "qvalue",   fdr_level = 0.05 ) print(cls) #> <eb_classification> #>   rule:           storey #>   fdr_level:      0.050 #>   direction:      upper #>   pi0:            0.392 #>   units:          97 #>   n_selected:     27 #>  #>   CD-78 reference rule numbers (do not conflate): #>     q-rule          = 27 #>     pi0=0.39 manual = 28 #>     monotone        = 30 #>     posterior-mean  = 19  # pi0 rounded to 4 decimals (DEC-197-2) — read via the classification's # canonical pi0 slot (resolve via known synonyms) pi0_val <- cls$pi0 %||% cls$pi0_hat %||% attr(cls, "pi0") %||% NA_real_ if (!is.na(pi0_val)) {   stopifnot(isTRUE(all.equal(pi0_val, round(pi0_val, 4), tolerance = 1e-15)))   cat("pi0 =", pi0_val, "(4-decimal exact)
") } else {   cat("(pi0 slot not directly exposed on classification object;
",       "see fit$prior for hyperparameters)
", sep = "") } #> pi0 = 0.3918 (4-decimal exact)

The lock is enforced #> operationally by `.githooks/pre-commit` (which aborts any commit touching these #> paths) and verified at test-time by `tests/testthat/test-frozen-checksums.R` #> (which recomputes SHA256 hashes against `inst/locked-core-checksums.txt`). #>  #> The frozen-files boundary preserves the v1.0.0 deconvolution-engine behaviour #> bit-exactly so that v2 statistical results reproduce v1 results without any #> new round of upstream MATLAB-reference validation. #>  #> ## The 12 frozen files #>  #> | # | Path | Role | #> |---|------|------| #> | 1 | `R/deconv-engine.R` | Top-level dispatcher for the log-spline deconvolution pipeline | #> | 2 | `R/deconv-spline.R` | B-spline basis construction and design-matrix builder | #> | 3 | `R/deconv-likelihood.R` | Marginal log-likelihood evaluation | #> | 4 | `R/deconv-penalty.R` | Roughness-penalty terms for the spline coefficients | # Conceptual — the actual checksum manifest is in #   inst/locked-core-checksums.txt # and is verified by a pre-commit hook + testthat fixture. manifest_path <- system.file("locked-core-checksums.txt", package = "ebrecipe") if (file.exists(manifest_path) && nzchar(manifest_path)) {   manifest <- readLines(manifest_path)   cat("HF1 manifest has", length(manifest), "lines (12 file SHA256 each)

") } #> HF1 manifest has 12 lines (12 file SHA256 each)

code: library(ebrecipe) data(krw_firms)  # === Monolithic recipe: one call === fit <- eb(   x       = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s       = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id = krw_firms$firm_id,   control = eb_control(fdr_threshold = 0.05) ) print(fit)  # === Stepwise recipe: explicit stages (recommended for CD-78) === race  <- eb_input(   theta_hat = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s         = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id   = krw_firms$firm_id ) diag  <- eb_diagnose(estimates = race) std   <- eb_standardize(estimates = race, diagnostic = diag) prior <- eb_deconvolve(estimates = std) post  <- eb_shrink(estimates = std, prior = prior) cls   <- eb_classify(   estimates = std,   posterior = post,   method    = "qvalue",   fdr_level = 0.05 )  length(selected_units(cls)) #> [1] 27  # Visualize the shrinkage plot(fit)

title: Run a complete empirical Bayes analysis — eb
text: eb() umbrella entry point Walters Ch 2 (6-stage discrimination pipeline). supports two input interfaces (vector via x/s, summary-data via formula/data/se) runs: validate inputs (eb_input()); precision-dependence diagnostics (Walters Ch 2.6); optional standardization (Walters Ch 2.6 eq. 55); prior fitting (Walters Ch 2.4 linear Ch 5 NP); posterior shrinkage (Walters Ch 5 eq. 8); optional FDR-controlled classification (Walters Ch 3). Standardization runs three hold: heteroskedastic = TRUE, control$standardize = TRUE, control$precision_model != "none". Otherwise function skips standardization proceeds supplied estimate scale. Per DEC-203, monolith explicit pipeline produce numerically identical eb_fit objects inputs. Choose monolith one-shot reports pipeline stage-level introspection per-stage overrides.

title: Run a complete empirical Bayes analysis — eb
text: Use eb() defaults trusted one-shot reports suffice. Use 6-stage pipeline (eb_input() -> eb_diagnose() -> eb_standardize() -> eb_deconvolve() -> eb_shrink() -> eb_classify()) introspection per-stage overrides. paths produce numerically identical results (DEC-203 lock).

title: Run a complete empirical Bayes analysis — eb
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  krw_small <- utils::head(krw_firms, 120)  # Linear path is fast and avoids the NP optimizer. fit <- eb(   x = krw_small$theta_hat_race,   s = krw_small$se_race,   unit_id = krw_small$firm_id,   method = "linear",   control = eb_control(standardize = FALSE, precision_model = "none") ) fit$method #> [1] "linear" fit$classification$n_selected #> [1] 19  # \donttest{ # NP deconvolution path; ~1-3 s on 120 firms with grid_size = 100. fit_np <- eb(   x = krw_small$theta_hat_race,   s = krw_small$se_race,   method = "deconv",   control = eb_control(n_grid = 100, penalty = "none", standardize = FALSE) ) fit_np$prior$method #> [1] "logspline" # }

text: prior eb_prior object residual scale r. Priors already transformed scale = "theta" rejected. s Full vector standard errors original \(\theta\) scale. empirical distribution s defines theta-scale pushforward; changing s changes returned prior. psi_1 First precision-dependence parameter \(\hat\psi_1\). additive model additive intercept \(\hat\psi_0\) carried forward standardization fit; multiplicative model slope intercept \(\hat\psi_1\). psi_2 Second precision-dependence parameter \(\hat\psi_2\) (log(s) coefficient). model Precision-dependence model; one "multiplicative" "additive".

title: Transform an r-scale prior to the original theta scale — eb_change_of_variables
text: eb_prior object theta scale, fields: method Inherited input r-scale prior. alpha Inherited free spline coefficients (carried bookkeeping; re-fit theta scale). support Numeric vector (length length(prior$support)) giving new \(\theta\) grid; strictly increasing. density Renormalized theta-scale density. V Always NULL – r-scale sandwich VCV dropped. hyperparameters Pushforward summaries: mu (\(\sum \theta \cdot \mathrm{mass}\)), sigma_theta, sigma_theta_sq (clipped 0 discretization rounding). scale Always "theta". spline_info original prior$spline_info augmented change_of_variables_model (chosen mapping) change_of_variables_n (= length(s)).

title: Transform an r-scale prior to the original theta scale — eb_change_of_variables
text: Implements theta-scale pushforward Walters Ch 2.6, inverse direction eb_standardize(). Mappings: Multiplicative: \(\theta = \exp(\hat\psi_1 + \hat\psi_2 \log s) \cdot r\). Additive: \(\theta = \hat\psi_1 + \exp(\hat\psi_2 \log s) \cdot r\) (psi_1 plays additive-intercept role). sandwich VCV input prior carried forward defined free spline coefficients original r scale transfer directly theta-scale object. delta-method standard errors required, call eb_delta_method() input r-scale prior calling eb_change_of_variables(). Computationally, function () converts discretized r-scale density grid masses, (ii) transforms masses chosen mapping across supplied s, (iii) snaps transformed masses onto common \(\theta\) grid via nearest-neighbor binning, (iv) renormalizes. discretized approximation pushforward density; expect smoothing artifacts support boundary.

title: Transform an r-scale prior to the original theta scale — eb_change_of_variables
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = utils::head(krw_firms$theta_hat_race, 80),   s = utils::head(krw_firms$se_race, 80) )  # \donttest{ # Heavy: requires eb_deconvolve() (~1-3 s on 80 firms with grid_size = 100). diag_fit <- eb_diagnose(est, precision_models = "multiplicative") std_est <- eb_standardize(est, model = "multiplicative",                           diagnostic = diag_fit) prior_r <- eb_deconvolve(std_est, grid_size = 100, penalty = "none") fit <- attr(std_est, "precision_fit")  prior_theta <- eb_change_of_variables(   prior_r,   s = std_est$original_s,   psi_1 = fit$psi_1,   psi_2 = fit$psi_2,   model = "multiplicative" )  prior_theta$scale #> [1] "theta" head(prior_theta$support) #> [1] 0.000000000 0.002702655 0.005405311 0.008107966 0.010810621 0.013513277 # }

Empty list "variance" requested. multiplicative NLLS fit (legacy v1 shape: psi_1, se_psi_1, psi_2, se_psi_2, r_squared, vcov, method) "multiplicative" precision_models; otherwise NULL. additive shape multiplicative additive model; NULL requested. conclusion Character scalar summarising test outcomes (e.g. "level dependence detected; strong evidence variance dependence").

title: Diagnose precision dependence in noisy estimates — eb_diagnose
text: Walters Ch 2.6 (eq. 55) motivates level test \(E[\hat\theta_j \mid s_j] = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \log s_j\); significance \(\beta_1\) indicates estimate magnitudes depend precision. Walters Ch 2.7 develops variance proxy regression \(E[(\hat\theta_j - \bar\theta)^2 - s_j^2 \mid s_j] = \gamma_0 + \gamma_1 \log s_j\) evidence prior-variance heteroskedasticity. regressions use HC1-robust standard errors (Stata-default convention) reported p-values heteroscedasticity-consistent. optional NLLS fits provide ready input eb_standardize() without re-fitting; psi_1, psi_2, r_squared fields stable across v2.0-v2.5 (see precision_fit()).

what: Decision tree – what the conclusion means
title: Diagnose precision dependence in noisy estimates — eb_diagnose
text: Level test significant -> multiplicative model: $$\hat\theta_j = \exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j) \cdot r_j.$$ Variance test significant -> additive model: $$\hat\theta_j = \psi_0 + s_j^{\psi_2} \cdot r_j.$$ Neither significant -> standardization needed; pass precision_model = "none" eb(). significant -> start multiplicative fit; compare R-squared additive fit pick larger. Inspect result$conclusion human-readable summary; multiplicative$r_squared additive$r_squared give comparison numbers.

title: Diagnose precision dependence in noisy estimates — eb_diagnose
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s         = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id   = krw_firms$firm_id )  diag_fit <- eb_diagnose(   est,   tests            = c("level", "variance"),   precision_models = c("multiplicative", "additive") )  diag_fit$conclusion #> [1] "level dependence detected; no strong evidence of variance dependence" diag_fit$level_test$p_value #> [1] 3.382649e-09 precision_fit(diag_fit, model = "multiplicative")$r_squared #> [1] 0.5953037

text: Already numeric vector \(\hat\theta_j\) \(s_j\) -> use eb_input(). student-level data need \(\hat\theta_j\) fixed-effect regression -> use eb_estimate_fe(). panel micro-level data one treatment contrast per group -> use eb_estimate_groups(). Need synthetic VAM data known truth testing -> use eb_simulate().

what: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
title: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
text: Compares raw-estimate posterior-mean mean squared error following Walters Ch 2.5. known latent truth, returns exact empirical MSE; theta_true = NULL, returns Walters-style proxy summary used package's replicated discrimination workflow.

title: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
code: eb_mse(posterior, theta_true = NULL)

title: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
text: posterior eb_posterior object. theta_true Optional numeric vector true latent effects, aligned posterior rows. omitted, function reports proxy summary instead exact latent-truth MSE.

title: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
text: named list five numeric scalars: mse_raw Truth-branch \(\overline{(\hat\theta_j - \theta_j)^2}\) proxy-branch \(\overline{s_j^2}\). Never NA valid input. mse_posterior Truth-branch \(\overline{(\tilde\theta_j - \theta_j)^2}\) proxy-branch \(\max(\hat\sigma_\theta^2 - \widehat{\mathrm{Var}}(\tilde\theta_j), 0)\). Floored 0 proxy branch design. Never NA. reduction 1 - ratio. Can negative posterior MSE exceeds raw MSE (rare; mostly mis-specified priors). ratio mse_posterior / mse_raw. mean_squared_adjustment \(\overline{(\hat\theta_j - \tilde\theta_j)^2}\); branch-independent shrinkage magnitude. Never NA.

title: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
text: Implements MSE comparison Walters Ch 2.5. Two branches: Truth branch (theta_true supplied): $$\mathrm{mse\_raw} = \overline{(\hat\theta_j - \theta_j)^2}, \quad \mathrm{mse\_posterior} = \overline{(\tilde\theta_j - \theta_j)^2}.$$ Proxy branch (theta_true = NULL): $$\mathrm{mse\_raw} = \overline{s_j^2}, \quad \mathrm{mse\_posterior} = \max\!\bigl(\hat\sigma_\theta^2 - \widehat{\mathrm{Var}}(\tilde\theta_j),\ 0\bigr),$$ \(\widehat{\mathrm{Var}}\) Bessel-corrected sample variance (stats::sd()^2). max(..., 0) floor intentional: prevents proxy going negative posterior-mean dispersion exceeds fitted prior variance. proxy branch assumes posterior means, measurement-error SDs, prior variance interpretable output scale; intended replicated Walters discrimination summaries. mean_squared_adjustment (\(\overline{(\hat\theta_j - \tilde\theta_j)^2}\)) reported branches measures shrinkage magnitude regardless branch.

title: Compare MSE before and after shrinkage — eb_mse
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  fit <- eb(   x = utils::head(krw_firms$theta_hat_race, 120),   s = utils::head(krw_firms$se_race, 120),   method = "linear",   output = "posterior",   control = eb_control(standardize = FALSE, precision_model = "none") )  post <- eb_shrink(fit$estimates, fit$prior, method = "linear") eb_mse(post) #> $mse_raw #> [1] 0.0003100908 #>  #> $mse_posterior #> [1] 0.0001412801 #>  #> $reduction #> [1] 0.5443913 #>  #> $ratio #> [1] 0.4556087 #>  #> $mean_squared_adjustment #> [1] 0.0001835061 #>

what: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
text: Estimates null proportion \(\hat\pi_0 \[0, 1]\) used package's q-value workflow. Implements Storey lambda-truncated ratio Walters 4-decimal replication contract; also accepts fixed value passthrough high-level callers.

title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
code: eb_pi0(p, lambda = 0.5, method = c("storey", "fixed"))

title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
text: p Numeric vector p-values \(p_j \[0, 1]\); finite. Length \(J\). lambda Storey threshold \(\lambda \[0, 1)\) method = "storey". method = "fixed", argument reused fixed null proportion (still clipped \([0, 1]\)). Default 0.50 per replication contract (DEC-197-2). method Null-proportion estimation method. One "storey" "fixed".

title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
text: named list three fields: pi0 Numeric scalar \([0, 1]\). Storey ratio (4-decimal rounded, clipped) method = "storey"; clipped lambda method = "fixed". Never NA. method Character: method argument used ("storey" "fixed"). lambda Numeric: lambda argument used (threshold Storey, fixed value "fixed").

title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
text: Walters Ch 3.4 eq. 102 defines Storey ratio. function applies package's replication contract: $$\hat\pi_0 = \mathrm{round}\!\left(\frac{\#\{p_j > \lambda\}}{J(1-\lambda)},\ 4\right),$$ clipped \([0, 1]\). 4-decimal rounding deliberate: KRW white-discrimination fixture (CD-78 anchor), contract preserves published \(\hat\pi_0 = 0.3918\) boundary associated 27-firm selection count \(\alpha = 0.05\). Departing rounding (e.g. using full double precision) shifts boundary count. method = "fixed", function estimate \(\hat\pi_0\); returns lambda clipped \([0, 1]\). branch intended high-level callers (eb(), eb_test()) read control$pi0_lambda forward . users need call eb_pi0() directly; eb_classify() calls internally contract default lambda = 0.50. public export provided diagnostics, sensitivity analysis (sweeping \(\lambda\)), reproduction Walters fixture boundaries.

title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
text: method = "storey" (default) – data-driven: estimate \(\hat\pi_0\) p. Use standalone calls eb_classify() default. method = "fixed" – passthrough: interpret lambda user-supplied fixed null proportion return (clipped \([0, 1]\)). Use parent function (e.g. eb() via control$pi0_lambda) plugs pre-decided value.

title: Estimate the null proportion \(\hat\pi_0\) from p-values — eb_pi0
code: # Standalone Storey estimate on a small fixture. eb_pi0(   p = c(0.01, 0.02, 0.10, 0.30, 0.60, 0.80),   lambda = 0.50,   method = "storey" ) #> $pi0 #> [1] 0.6667 #>  #> $method #> [1] "storey" #>  #> $lambda #> [1] 0.5 #>   # Use inside the KRW race classification (the published path). data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe") z <- krw_firms$theta_hat_race / krw_firms$se_race p_upper <- stats::pnorm(-z) eb_pi0(p_upper, lambda = 0.50, method = "storey") #> $pi0 #> [1] 0.3918 #>  #> $method #> [1] "storey" #>  #> $lambda #> [1] 0.5 #>   # Sensitivity sweep over lambda. lambdas <- seq(0.30, 0.70, by = 0.05) vapply(lambdas, function(l) eb_pi0(p_upper, lambda = l)$pi0, numeric(1)) #> [1] 0.3976 0.4124 0.4296 0.4686 0.3918 0.3895 0.3608 0.3535 0.3436  # Fixed passthrough. eb_pi0(p_upper, lambda = 0.40, method = "fixed") #> $pi0 #> [1] 0.4 #>  #> $method #> [1] "fixed" #>  #> $lambda #> [1] 0.4 #>

what: Broom and ggplot2 methods for eb_posterior objects — autoplot.eb_posterior
title: Broom and ggplot2 methods for eb_posterior objects — autoplot.eb_posterior
text: Draws posterior shrinkage map: pre-shrinkage theta_hat x-axis vs posterior.mean y-axis, dashed y = x reference. Points falling away diagonal pulled toward prior mean. tidy() returns one row per unit. symmetry tidy.eb_fit(), estimate carries posterior mean std.error carries original observed .s. Per documented design decision, shrinkage.weight variance.ratio columns emitted: linear path populates shrinkage.weight; nonparametric path populates variance.ratio; inactive column carries NA consumers can rbind-stack tidy outputs across fit methods. Returns one-row data frame method, nobs, mean shrinkage weight (linear path) mean variance ratio (NP path), posterior-mean range. augment() returns per-unit posterior table joined input data (supplied). Columns added: .fitted (posterior mean), .resid (theta_hat - posterior mean), .posterior_sd, .shrinkage_weight, .variance_ratio (dual-column). Equivalent .data.frame(tidy(model)). result includes dual-column posterior schema (shrinkage.weight ∪ variance.ratio).

text: estimates eb_estimates object. prior eb_prior object carries standardization metadata prior$spline_info (typically standardize -> deconvolve workflow). units Optional subset unit indices estimates. Ignored grid supplied. grid Optional override grid. supplied, first two columns interpreted theta-scale \(\hat\theta\) \(s\) values regardless column names. ... Additional arguments reserved future use.

title: Evaluate posterior decision surfaces on a theta-s grid — eb_posterior_grid
text: base data frame six columns: .theta_hat Theta-scale evaluation points. .s Theta-scale standard errors. .posterior_mean Nonparametric posterior mean theta scale (residual-scale NP, back-transformed). Primary column. .posterior_mean_linear Linear shrinkage theta-scale grid directly. .posterior_mean_linear_alt Linear shrinkage residual scale, back-transformed. .p_value Upper-tail normal reference \(1 - \Phi(\hat\theta_j / s_j)\); screening statistic .

title: Evaluate posterior decision surfaces on a theta-s grid — eb_posterior_grid
text: Implements decision-surface contract Walters Ch 3.3. MATLAB-matching workflow : Read supplied theta-scale grid (fall back estimates$original_theta_hat/original_s). Use prior$spline_info transform grid residual scale via eb_standardize()'s mapping. Compute nonparametric posterior mean residual scale (Walters Ch 5 eq. 8). Back-transform posterior mean theta scale. standardized estimates grid = NULL, function automatically falls back original_theta_hat/original_s, public grid always interpreted original \(\theta\) scale. grid supplied, units ignored. Output columns distinct meanings: .posterior_mean – nonparametric posterior mean, computed residual scale back-transformed \(\theta\). PRIMARY column. .posterior_mean_linear – method--moments linear shrinkage applied directly theta-scale grid. .posterior_mean_linear_alt – method--moments linear shrinkage applied residual scale back-transformed (consistency check .posterior_mean_linear). .p_value – upper-tail normal reference \(1 - \Phi(\hat\theta_j / s_j)\), included SCREENING statistic , posterior probability q-value.

title: Evaluate posterior decision surfaces on a theta-s grid — eb_posterior_grid
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = utils::head(krw_firms$theta_hat_race, 80),   s = utils::head(krw_firms$se_race, 80) )  # \donttest{ # Heavy: requires eb_deconvolve() (~1-3 s on 80 firms with grid_size = 100). diag_fit <- eb_diagnose(est, precision_models = "multiplicative") std_est <- eb_standardize(est, model = "multiplicative",                           diagnostic = diag_fit) prior_r <- eb_deconvolve(std_est, grid_size = 100, penalty = "none")  grid_out <- eb_posterior_grid(   estimates = std_est,   prior = prior_r,   grid = data.frame(     theta_hat = c(0.00, 0.05, 0.10),     s = c(0.05, 0.08, 0.10)   ) )  grid_out[, c(".theta_hat", ".posterior_mean", ".p_value")] #>   .theta_hat .posterior_mean  .p_value #> 1       0.00      0.01946057 0.5000000 #> 2       0.05      0.03787844 0.2659855 #> 3       0.10      0.05890488 0.1586553 # }

what: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
text: Converts eb_posterior object long-format ranking table attaches score, midrank, change original estimate rank unit. Supports posterior-mean ranking (EB shrunk default), raw-estimate ranking (shrinkage; useful benchmark), q-value ranking reuses eb_classify() FDR contract.

title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
code: eb_rank(   posterior,   method = c("posterior_mean", "qvalue", "estimate", "posterior_probability"),   target = NULL,   n_sim = 1000,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
text: posterior eb_posterior object (e.g. eb_shrink() eb() output = "posterior"). method Ranking criterion. One "posterior_mean" (default), "qvalue", "estimate", "posterior_probability" (reserved). target Optional ranking target \(\tau\) (reserved "posterior_probability" rule; currently unused). n_sim Number simulations stochastic ranking methods. Currently unused; reserved "posterior_probability". Default 1000. seed Optional random seed stochastic methods. Currently unused. ... Additional arguments forwarded eb_classify() method = "qvalue" (e.g. direction, pi0_method, threshold_b, fdr_level); controlling estimates, posterior, method, frontier arguments reserved eb_rank() silently dropped.

title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
text: data frame one row per unit following columns: .unit_id Unit identifier posterior$posterior$.unit_id, posterior$estimates$unit_id, seq_len(J) fallback. Type matches source. .score Numeric score used current ranking (posterior mean, raw estimate, q-value). .rank Numeric midrank current method (average tie-handling, values may non-integer ties present). .rank_original Numeric midrank raw estimate \(\hat\theta_j\), largest first. .rank_change Numeric .rank_original - .rank; positive means active rule promoted unit relative raw ordering. .method Character: method argument used, repeated J times. Length \(J\) = number posterior rows; NA rows introduced.

title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
text: Walters Ch 3.5 (posterior-mean rank) Ch 3.4 eq. 103 (q-value rank) anchor criteria. Ties broken average midrank (base::rank(..., ties.method = "average")), reported ranks can non-integer several units share score. method = "qvalue", function calls eb_classify() internally frontier = FALSE forwards extra ... arguments (pi0_method, threshold_b, direction). "posterior_probability" criterion (rank \(P(\theta_j > \tau \mid \mathrm{data})\) target \(\tau\)) reserved future enhancement; currently raises typed error.

title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
text: method = "posterior_mean" – default; ranks \(E[\theta_j \mid \mathrm{data}]\). method = "estimate" – ranks raw \(\hat\theta_j\) (shrinkage). method = "qvalue" – ranks FDR-controlled q-values. method = "posterior_probability" – ranks \(P(\theta_j > \tau \mid \mathrm{data})\); requires target = \tau. Reserved; raises error current implementation.

title: Rank units by posterior summaries — eb_rank
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  fit <- eb(   x = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id = krw_firms$firm_id,   method = "linear",   output = "posterior",   control = eb_control(standardize = FALSE, precision_model = "none") ) post <- eb_shrink(fit$estimates, fit$prior, method = "linear")  # Rank by posterior mean (default). rk_pm <- eb_rank(post, method = "posterior_mean") head(rk_pm) #>   .unit_id       .score .rank .rank_original .rank_change        .method #> 1        1 0.0344680562     8           17.0          9.0 posterior_mean #> 2        2 0.0215962429    39           40.5          1.5 posterior_mean #> 3        3 0.0284215518    24           20.0         -4.0 posterior_mean #> 4        4 0.0125901272    69           71.0          2.0 posterior_mean #> 5        5 0.0260209331    29           28.0         -1.0 posterior_mean #> 6        7 0.0005414614    89           91.0          2.0 posterior_mean  # Rank by q-value (delegates to eb_classify internally). rk_q <- eb_rank(post, method = "qvalue", direction = "upper") head(rk_q[order(rk_q$.rank), ]) #>    .unit_id       .score .rank .rank_original .rank_change .method #> 62       75 3.113724e-05     1              1            0  qvalue #> 15       16 4.238539e-03     2             11            9  qvalue #> 28       31 5.546664e-03     3              3            0  qvalue #> 61       74 6.012440e-03     4              2           -2  qvalue #> 1         1 1.182817e-02     5             17           12  qvalue #> 93      119 1.183952e-02     6              5           -1  qvalue

title: Compute unit-level reliability weights — eb_reliability
text: Returns linear empirical-Bayes reliability weights \(\lambda_j = \sigma_\theta^2 / (\sigma_\theta^2 + s_j^2)\) per Walters Ch 2.4. Larger measurement-error standard deviations imply smaller \(\lambda_j\) aggressive shrinkage toward prior mean.

title: Compute unit-level reliability weights — eb_reliability
code: eb_reliability(estimates, prior)

title: Compute unit-level reliability weights — eb_reliability
text: estimates eb_estimates object supplying \(s_j\). prior eb_prior object supplying \(\sigma_\theta^2\).

title: Compute unit-level reliability weights — eb_reliability
text: numeric vector reliability weights \(\lambda_j \[0, 1]\), one per unit, order estimates$theta_hat. Length Equal length(estimates$theta_hat). Range entry lies (0, 1] finite, strictly positive s finite, non-negative \(\sigma_\theta^2\); degenerate \(\sigma_\theta^2 = 0\) yields weights 0. Names Set .character(estimates$unit_id) unit_id non-NULL; otherwise result unnamed. NA rule Never NA valid input.

title: Compute unit-level reliability weights — eb_reliability
text: Implements linear EB reliability weight Walters Ch 2.4: $$\lambda_j = \frac{\sigma_\theta^2}{\sigma_\theta^2 + s_j^2},$$ \(\sigma_\theta^2\) read prior$hyperparameters (via sigma_theta_sq, falling back sigma_sq sigma_theta^2). Larger measurement-error standard deviations imply smaller reliability aggressive shrinkage toward prior mean. \(\lambda_j \[0, 1]\). linear-EB-counterpart .shrinkage_weight column emitted eb_shrink() linear path; nonparametric path eb_shrink() collapse single \(\lambda_j\).

title: Compute posterior shrinkage estimates — eb_shrink
text: Combine eb_estimates object eb_prior posterior mean summaries. nonparametric path integrates supplied prior likelihood (Walters Ch 5 eq. 8); linear path applies closed-form linear EB shrinkage (Walters Ch 2.4) ignores prior calculation. standardized workflows, unstandardize = TRUE (default) returns posterior original \(\theta\) scale.

code: eb_shrink(   estimates,   prior,   method = c("nonparametric", "linear"),   unstandardize = TRUE,   ... )

title: Compute posterior shrinkage estimates — eb_shrink
text: estimates eb_estimates object. prior eb_prior object. method Shrinkage method. "nonparametric" uses supplied prior; "linear" uses method--moments linear shrinkage estimates. linear path keeps prior returned object bookkeeping otherwise use . unstandardize Logical. TRUE estimates$standardized = TRUE, uses standardization metadata stored prior$spline_info return .theta_hat, .s, .posterior_mean original \(\theta\) scale. FALSE, columns remain working (typically residual) scale. ... Additional arguments reserved future use.

title: Compute posterior shrinkage estimates — eb_shrink
text: eb_posterior object whose posterior data frame nine columns: .unit_id Unit identifier (seq_along() absent). .theta_hat Estimate output scale (theta scale unstandardize = TRUE inputs standardized; otherwise working scale). .s Standard error matching scale. .posterior_mean Posterior mean scale .theta_hat. .posterior_sd Currently placeholder NA_real_. .shrinkage_weight Linear-path \(w_j \\eqn{[0, 1]}\); NA_real_ NP path. .variance_ratio NP-path \(V_j^* / s_j^2\) (unclipped); NA_real_ linear path. .ci_lower Currently placeholder NA_real_. .ci_upper Currently placeholder NA_real_. .shrinkage_weight .variance_ratio MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE per row.

title: Compute posterior shrinkage estimates — eb_shrink
text: Implements shrinkage paths Walters Ch 2.4 (linear closed form) Walters Ch 5 eq. 8 (nonparametric posterior). two paths emit MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE shrinkage columns: Linear path: .shrinkage_weight = \(w_j \\eqn{[0, 1]}\) (data weight per Walters Ch 2.1 eq. 12); .variance_ratio NA. defensive bound enforces \(w_j \\eqn{[0, 1]}\) errors violation (frozen-engine invariant). Nonparametric path: .variance_ratio = \(V_j^* / s_j^2\) computed \(J \times M\) grid weights, clipped (may exceed 1 tails non-Gaussian priors per Worksheet B.1); .shrinkage_weight NA. dual-column convention intentional: single posterior table can carry either kind weight, downstream consumers branch column non-NA. Posterior-SD CI columns (.posterior_sd, .ci_lower, .ci_upper) currently placeholders filled NA. unstandardize = FALSE, output remains current working scale; debugging residual-scale workflows, user-facing display.

title: Compute posterior shrinkage estimates — eb_shrink
text: method = "nonparametric" (default NP eb_deconvolve prior): full posterior via integration; emits .variance_ratio (unbounded; may exceed 1 tails per Worksheet B.1). method = "linear": closed-form linear EB; emits .shrinkage_weight \([0, 1]\). Columns MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE per row (dual-column posterior).

title: Compute posterior shrinkage estimates — eb_shrink
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = utils::head(krw_firms$theta_hat_race, 80),   s = utils::head(krw_firms$se_race, 80) )  # Linear path -- closed form, fast. linear_prior <- eb_deconvolve(est, grid_size = 30, penalty = "none") post_lin <- eb_shrink(est, linear_prior, method = "linear") head(post_lin$posterior[, c(".theta_hat", ".posterior_mean",                             ".shrinkage_weight")]) #>     .theta_hat .posterior_mean .shrinkage_weight #> 1  0.046957124    0.0352492780         0.5381604 #> 2  0.022000000    0.0218293205         0.5660729 #> 3  0.042161614    0.0291491040         0.3669403 #> 4  0.005708306    0.0124749639         0.5743800 #> 5  0.034077112    0.0265822542         0.3989905 #> 6 -0.010182468    0.0001042363         0.6764081  # \donttest{ # NP path on standardized inputs (~1-3 s on 80 firms with grid_size = 100). diag_fit <- eb_diagnose(est, precision_models = "multiplicative") std_est  <- eb_standardize(est, model = "multiplicative",                            diagnostic = diag_fit) prior_r  <- eb_deconvolve(std_est, grid_size = 100, penalty = "none") post_np  <- eb_shrink(std_est, prior_r, method = "nonparametric",                       unstandardize = TRUE) head(post_np$posterior[, c(".theta_hat", ".posterior_mean",                            ".variance_ratio")]) #>     .theta_hat .posterior_mean .variance_ratio #> 1  0.046957124     0.027150060       0.1875210 #> 2  0.022000000     0.021490209       0.2038323 #> 3  0.042161614     0.041582396       0.2099671 #> 4  0.005708306     0.016050444       0.4248029 #> 5  0.034077112     0.036370234       0.2535444 #> 6 -0.010182468     0.006598869       0.3625261 # }

title: Compute conditional linear empirical Bayes shrinkage — eb_shrink_conditional
text: estimates eb_estimates object. Must carry covariates data frame containing every variable named formula. formula One-sided formula defining conditioning covariates (e.g. ~ charter). Variables looked estimates$covariates. sigma_sq Optional residual signal variance override \(\sigma_r^2\). omitted, uses conditional variance estimate implied formula. control Control settings eb_control(). Currently validated API consistency otherwise unused path. ... Additional arguments reserved future use.

title: Compute conditional linear empirical Bayes shrinkage — eb_shrink_conditional
text: eb_posterior object whose posterior data frame nine columns: .unit_id Unit identifier (seq_along() absent). .theta_hat Observed estimate theta scale. .s Standard error theta scale. .prior_mean Conditional prior mean \(Z_j' \hat\mu\). .posterior_mean Posterior mean \(w_j \hat\theta_j + (1 - w_j) Z_j' \hat\mu\). .posterior_sd Currently placeholder NA_real_. .shrinkage_weight Linear weight \(w_j = \sigma_r^2 / (\sigma_r^2 + s_j^2)\) \([0, 1]\). .ci_lower Currently placeholder NA_real_. .ci_upper Currently placeholder NA_real_. method returned object always "conditional_linear". prior eb_prior method = "normal" carrying conditional hyperparameters.

title: Compute conditional linear empirical Bayes shrinkage — eb_shrink_conditional
text: Implements conditional linear EB path Walters Ch 4.3. nonparametric routine; public contract conditional normal-prior approximation: Unit-specific prior mean \(Z_j' \hat\mu\) conditioning model. Common residual signal variance \(\sigma_r^2\). Linear shrinkage weight \(w_j = \sigma_r^2 / (\sigma_r^2 + s_j^2)\), closed-form posterior \(\tilde\theta_j = w_j \hat\theta_j + (1 - w_j) Z_j' \hat\mu\). Always operates observed \(\theta\) scale. Unlike eb_shrink() standardize/unstandardize stage. defensive bound enforces \(w_j \\eqn{[0, 1]}\) errors violation (tripwire upstream regressions conditional hyperparameter fit).

title: Simulate value-added data with known ground truth — eb_simulate
text: Generates synthetic value-added (VAM) panel known unit effects \(\theta_j\) student-level outcomes \(y_{ij}\), returning three-table eb_sim object (students, schools, dgp). Designed tutorials, regression tests, end--end EB workflow verification access truth essential.

code: eb_simulate(   n_units = 50,   n_obs = 2500,   sigma_theta = 0.2,   design = c("balanced", "unbalanced"),   groups = NULL,   seed = NULL,   J = NULL,   N = NULL )

title: Simulate value-added data with known ground truth — eb_simulate
text: n_units Positive integer; number units (schools) \(J\) simulate. Default 50. n_obs Positive integer; total number observations (students) \(N\) simulate. Default 2500. sigma_theta Non-negative numeric scalar; standard deviation latent unit signal \(\theta_j \sim N(0, \sigma_\theta^2)\). Default 0.20. design Character scalar; "balanced" allocates roughly equal numbers students per unit. "unbalanced" uses discrete-choice assignment rule driven school-specific utility shocks. groups Optional named list. current public schema supports charter sub-list numeric share (\([0, 1]\)) numeric boost (additive mean shift \(\theta_j\) charter units). seed Optional integer random seed. supplied, function saves restores .GlobalEnv$.Random.seed exit caller's RNG stream disturbed. J Optional alias n_units, kept workshop-style notation (Walters writes \(J\) number units). Overrides n_units supplied. N Optional alias n_obs, kept workshop-style notation (Walters writes \(N\) number observations). Overrides n_obs supplied.

title: Simulate value-added data with known ground truth — eb_simulate
text: eb_sim object (S3 list) three components: students Data frame – observation-level records (student_id, school_id, x covariate, theta_true, y outcome, charter, group); always \(N\) rows. schools Data frame – unit-level latent truth assignment components (school_id, theta, theta_true, delta, gamma, charter, group, n_students); always \(J\) rows. dgp Named list – compact record simulation settings used generate draw (n_units, n_obs, sigma_theta, design, groups, seed, plus charter-related counts). Sufficient reproduce draw exactly.

title: Simulate value-added data with known ground truth — eb_simulate
text: data-generating process draws unit effects $$\theta_j \sim N(0, \sigma_\theta^2),$$ student-level covariate \(x_i \sim N(0,1)\), outcomes $$y_{ij} = \theta_{j()} + x_i + \varepsilon_i, \quad \varepsilon_i \sim N(0,1).$$ matches canonical homoskedastic VAM setup Walters Ch 2.2 (eq. 5-6). unbalanced design, school assignment generated school-specific utility components \(\delta_j\), \(\gamma_j\), Gumbel shocks, produces realistic unequal school sizes. optional groups$charter block adds additive mean shift boost \(\theta_j\) share fraction units, enabling group-conditional shrinkage demos (Walters Ch 6.2). Although eb_simulate() returns eb_sim object rather eb_estimates object directly, students table canonical input eb_estimate_fe() end--end shrinkage workflows; reason grouped estimate-layer constructors eb_estimates family.

title: Simulate value-added data with known ground truth — eb_simulate

title: Standardize estimates to remove precision dependence — eb_standardize

Returns new eb_estimates object residual scale, preserving originals later back-transformation.

title: Standardize estimates to remove precision dependence — eb_standardize
text: estimates eb_estimates object. model Precision-dependence model use; one "multiplicative" "additive". diagnostic Optional precomputed eb_diagnostic. Supplying reuses fitted diagnostic models eb_diagnose() instead refitting . start Optional starting values NLLS optimizer. ... Additional arguments reserved future implementation.

title: Standardize estimates to remove precision dependence — eb_standardize
text: eb_estimates object standardized residual scale, following fields: theta_hat Standardized residual-scale estimates. s Standardized residual-scale standard errors. original_theta_hat pre-standardization estimates (always preserved). original_s pre-standardization standard errors. standardized Logical scalar; always TRUE returned object. standardization_model Character scalar "multiplicative" "additive". hyperparameters Method--moments hyperparameters recomputed residual scale. attr(x, "precision_fit") fitted NLLS object \(\psi_0\), \(\psi_1\), \(\psi_2\), robust VCV, uncentered pseudo-\(R^2\). attr(x, "diagnostic") full eb_diagnostic bundle. unit_id, n, covariates (present) passed unchanged.

title: Standardize estimates to remove precision dependence — eb_standardize
text: Implements multiplicative additive precision-dependence models Walters Ch 2.6 eq. 55. two models correspond different stories heteroskedasticity enters \(\hat\theta_j\): Multiplicative: \(\hat\theta_j = \exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j) \cdot r_j\); estimates standard errors rescaled \(\exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j)\). Additive: \(\hat\theta_j = \psi_0 + s_j^{\psi_2} r_j\) first removing common intercept \(\psi_0\); remaining variance pattern modelled function \(\log s_j\). Fitted parameters stored attr(x, "precision_fit") full diagnostic bundle attr(x, "diagnostic"). reported r_squared uncentered Walters-style pseudo-\(R^2\), centered OLS \(R^2\): multiplicative path, \(1 - \mathrm{SSR}/\sum \hat\theta_j^2\); additive path, \(1 - \mathrm{SSR}/\sum y_j^2\) working response \(y_j = (\hat\theta_j - \psi_0)^2 - s_j^2\). definitions match published Walters NLLS targets part replication contract. Standardization reversible: returned object stores original_theta_hat, original_s, standardization_model, eb_shrink() (unstandardize = TRUE) can map posterior summaries back theta scale.

title: Standardize estimates to remove precision dependence — eb_standardize
text: model = "multiplicative" – level test eb_diagnose() significant. Transform: \(r_j = \hat\theta_j / \exp(\hat\psi_1 + \hat\psi_2 \log s_j)\). model = "additive" – variance test significant. Transform: \(r_j = (\hat\theta_j - \hat\psi_0) / s_j^{\hat\psi_2}\).

title: Standardize estimates to remove precision dependence — eb_standardize
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = utils::head(krw_firms$theta_hat_race, 120),   s = utils::head(krw_firms$se_race, 120) )  diag_fit <- eb_diagnose(est, precision_models = "multiplicative") std_est <- eb_standardize(est, model = "multiplicative", diagnostic = diag_fit)  std_est$standardized #> [1] TRUE std_est$standardization_model #> [1] "multiplicative"

title: Wrap a deconvolveR result as an eb_prior — from_deconvolveR
text: object deconvolveR::deconv() result list. Must contain stats component columns named theta (tau) g (tg). Optional cached fields (mle, cov, Q, P, S, cov.g, loglik, statsFunction) preserved resulting prior present. ... Currently recognized: sigma (positive numeric scalar, default 1) converting standardized support \(\theta\) scale homoskedastic normal errors; scale (one "theta"/"z", default "theta") choosing support scale stored prior. arguments reserved.

title: Wrap a deconvolveR result as an eb_prior — from_deconvolveR
text: eb_prior object method = "deconvolver" following public fields: method Character scalar – always "deconvolver" constructor. alpha Numeric vector – imported mle (spline coefficients); numeric(0) absent. support Numeric vector – grid points chosen scale. scale = "theta", equal tau * sigma; scale = "z", equal tau. Strictly increasing; never NA. density Numeric vector – normalized prior density support, integrating 1. Never NA. V Numeric matrix NULL – deconvolveR cov matrix present. hyperparameters Named list discrete moments mu (\(\sum_k \tau_k g_k\)), sigma_theta, sigma_theta_sq. Recomputed imported support mass; never NA. scale Character scalar – "theta" "z" mirroring scale argument. spline_info Named list caching deconvolveR-specific quantities (backend = "deconvolveR", deconvolveR_tau, deconvolveR_mass, deconvolveR_Q, deconvolveR_P, deconvolveR_S, deconvolveR_cov_g, deconvolveR_loglik, deconvolveR_stats_function, sigma) as_deconvolveR() can reconstruct original list losslessly.

title: Wrap a deconvolveR result as an eb_prior — from_deconvolveR
text: bridge assumes homoskedastic-normal convention used method = "deconvolver" path eb_deconvolve(): deconvolveR support tau standardized scale \(z = \hat\theta / \sigma\). scale = "theta", function rescales support \(\theta\) scale multiplying sigma; scale = "z" keeps standardized scale (Walters Ch 5.4 – Efron 2016 discrete-spline formulation). Hyperparameters recomputed discrete moments $$\mu = \sum_k \tau_k g_k, \quad       \sigma_\theta^2 = \sum_k (\tau_k - \mu)^2 g_k$$ resulting prior carries first- second-moment summaries compatible native logspline backend. native ebrecipe optimization context (e.g., variance-match penalty trace) reconstructed – comparison object, proof two backends fit identical models.

what: Decision tree – when to use which prior bridge
title: Wrap a deconvolveR result as an eb_prior — from_deconvolveR
text: Use from_deconvolveR() wrap deconvolveR::deconv() result ebrecipe prior. Use as_deconvolveR() inverse coercion. Use eb_deconvolve() method = "deconvolver" end--end fitting deconvolveR.

title: Wrap a deconvolveR result as an eb_prior — from_deconvolveR
code: # Wrap a tiny synthetic deconvolveR result. raw <- list(   mle = c(0.1, -0.2),   stats = cbind(     theta = c(-1, 0, 1),     g     = c(0.2, 0.6, 0.2)   ) ) prior <- from_deconvolveR(raw, sigma = 0.5, scale = "theta") prior$scale #> [1] "theta" prior$hyperparameters$mu #> [1] 0 range(prior$support) #> [1] -0.5  0.5  # Round-trip back to a deconvolveR-shaped structure. bridge <- as_deconvolveR(prior) identical(prior$scale, "theta") #> [1] TRUE

title: Extract the precision-dependence fit from an EB workflow object — precision_fit
text: v2 typed accessor precision-dependence NLLS fit embedded eb_estimates, eb_diagnostic, eb_fit object. fit characterises estimates depend standard errors via either multiplicative model \(\hat\theta_j = \exp(\psi_1 + \psi_2 \log s_j) \cdot r_j\) additive model \(\hat\theta_j = \psi_0 + s_j^{\psi_2} \cdot r_j\). Replaces v1 attr(x, "precision_fit") pattern typed, class-dispatched accessor.

title: Extract the precision-dependence fit from an EB workflow object — precision_fit
text: Either: eb_precision_fit object (v2.1 wraps legacy shape) carrying class "eb_precision_fit" fields . v1-shape NLLS list psi_1, se_psi_1, psi_2, se_psi_2, r_squared, vcov, method – returned verbatim v2.0 transition. NULL precision fit attached (e.g. eb_diagnostic built precision_models = character(0), eb_fit standardization disabled).

title: Extract the precision-dependence fit from an EB workflow object — precision_fit
text: v2-NEW typed accessor per redesign Step 2.5. Methods dispatched input class: precision_fit.eb_estimates, precision_fit.eb_diagnostic, precision_fit.eb_fit. default method raises typed-class error. Walters Ch 2.6 eq. 55 (multiplicative) Ch 2.7 (additive) define \(\psi\) parameters returned. R-squared values support model-comparison branch eb_diagnose() decision tree.

title: Extract the precision-dependence fit from an EB workflow object — precision_fit
text: underlying object stores fit v1-shape NLLS list (elements psi_1, se_psi_1, psi_2, se_psi_2, r_squared, vcov, method), accessor returns list verbatim – v1 contract preserved. v2.1 wrap legacy shape proper eb_precision_fit object created via new_eb_precision_fit(); user code reading shared field names ($psi_1, $psi_2, $r_squared) works unchanged across transition.

title: Extract the precision-dependence fit from an EB workflow object — precision_fit
code: data("krw_firms", package = "ebrecipe")  est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = krw_firms$theta_hat_race,   s         = krw_firms$se_race,   unit_id   = krw_firms$firm_id ) diag <- eb_diagnose(   est,   precision_models = c("multiplicative", "additive") )  fit_mul <- precision_fit(diag, model = "multiplicative") fit_mul$psi_1 #> [1] 2.523751 fit_mul$r_squared #> [1] 0.5953037  # Default selection (multiplicative if available, else additive). precision_fit(diag)$method #> [1] "nls"

code: residual_est <- eb_input(   theta_hat = c(-0.10, 0.05, 0.20, 0.35),   s = c(0.20, 0.20, 0.20, 0.20) ) prior <- eb_deconvolve(   residual_est,   penalty = "fixed",   penalty_value = 0.03,   characteristic = "male" )  pred <- predict(prior, x = c(0.00, 0.10), s = c(0.20, 0.20)) pred[, c(".theta_hat", ".posterior_mean")] #>   .theta_hat .posterior_mean #> 1        0.0    -0.007800811 #> 2        0.1     0.001200351

Pre-release 단계로, 정식 1.0.0 release 는 precomputed cache 도입 및 CRAN-ready submission 시점까지 보류.

title: ebrecipe 0.5.0
text: 10-vignette 구조 확립. 응용 트랙 5 편 (a1-getting-started → a5-visualization-cookbook) + 방법론 트랙 5 편 (m1-eb-recipe-foundations → m5-replication--reproducibility) 의 dual-track 구조로 전면 재편성. 각 vignette 은 ~165–250 source line 의 자기-완결적 walkthrough 로, 응용 트랙은 workflow 중심 (estimate → denoise → decide), 방법론 트랙은 수식 + 증명 + 검증 invariant 중심. Hex sticker 채택. EB shrinkage 의 시각적 은유 (raw dots → posterior cluster) 를 표현한 디자인. man/figures/logo.svg + man/figures/logo.png 양쪽 형식 제공. pkgdown site. _pkgdown.yml 신규 작성, 47 export reference index, 10 vignette articles, navbar 2-track grouping. https://joonho112.github.io/ebrecipe/ 으로 deploy 가능. cd78_selection_count() 단일 source truth 도입. CD-78 canonical 27 (DEC-197-2) 결정값을 helper 함수로 추출하여 vignettes/_setup.R, vignettes/companion-helpers.R, inst/scripts/ 의 mirror 위치에 byte-identical 으로 유지. Vignette + README + test 가 모두 동일한 정수를 참조.

title: ebrecipe 0.5.0
text: 기존 4 vignette 삭제. vignettes/ebrecipe.Rmd, discrimination.Rmd, school-vam.Rmd, visualization.Rmd 가 모두 제거되고 신규 a1–a5 / m1–m5 으로 대체됨. 외부에서 위 vignette URL 을 직접 참조하던 경우 신규 slug 로 redirect 필요. DESCRIPTION Suggests 축소. 미사용 의존성 ggdist, rlang, vctrs 3 개를 Suggests 에서 제거.

title: ebrecipe 0.5.0
text: cd78_selection_count() — CD-78 canonical 27 을 단일 정수로 반환하는 헬퍼. vignettes/_setup.R + vignettes/companion-helpers.R + inst/scripts/ 3 위치에 byte-identical 으로 유지. 신규 테스트 tests/testthat/test-cd78-invariant.R 가 expect_identical(., 27L) 과 mirror parity 양쪽을 검증. _pkgdown.yml — pkgdown site configuration. 2-track navbar, custom palette, 47 reference grouping, articles index. vignettes/_setup.R + companion-helpers.R — 10 vignette 공유 preamble. library(ebrecipe), ggplot2 theme, dataset fixture path, helper 함수 등 일괄 제공. vignettes/references.bib — 17 entry bibliography. Walters (2024), Efron (2012, 2014, 2019), Stone (1990), Koenker–Mizera (2014), Kline–Walters (2021), Chetty–Friedman–Rockoff (2014) 등 핵심 인용 정비.

title: ebrecipe 0.5.0
text: README 5-분 예제 정상화. 기존 eb_classify(..., posterior = fit$posterior) 가 dim/scale mismatch 로 즉시 에러하던 문제를 stepwise pipeline (eb_deconvolve → eb_shrink → eb_classify) 으로 교체. README 의 인용 단락이 그대로 복사하여 R session 에서 실행 가능. m4 vignette CD-78 chunk 정정. m4 의 selected-units chunk 가 19 를 산출하면서 같은 페이지에서 Expected: 27 을 표시하던 모순을 stepwise eb_classify(method = "qvalue") 로 교정. stopifnot(identical(n_selected, cd78_selection_count())) 로 strict 검증. vignettes/references.bib DOI 정정. 4 entry 의 DOI 가 unrelated paper 로 resolve 되던 문제를 정정 (KRW QJE 2022, Angrist et al. QJE 2017, Laird JASA 1978, Efron Biometrika 2014). R/plot-mixing-distribution.R @examples: scale = "theta" (없는 레벨) 를 scale = "r" 로 수정. R/plot-posterior-overlay.R @examples: \donttest{} 블록을 \dontrun{} 로 교체 (R CMD check policy). tests/testthat/test-frozen-checksums.R: 설치된 패키지 컨텍스트 (source R/ 부재) 에서 graceful skip 처리.

README.md 완전 재작성 — 270 line, 8+1 section (Overview, Install, 5-minute example, Two interfaces, Track structure, Data, Status & scope, References, BibTeX) + hex header + 작동하는 5-분 예제. Bibliography 정비 — vignettes/references.bib 17 entries.

NAMESPACE ↔︎ man/*.Rd ↔︎ docs/reference/ 의 3-way parity 가 47/47/47 로 검증됨 (test-namespace-ledger.R). Build hygiene — .Rbuildignore 에 internal artifact 디렉터리 제외 entries 추가. 미사용 Suggests 3 개 제거. Helper mirror — vignettes/_setup.R 와 companion-helpers.R 의 정본을 inst/scripts/ 에도 복사하여 vignette 빌드 시 system.file() 로 발견 가능. parity guard 테스트 신설. Test suite 확장 — 2,395 PASS / 0 FAIL / 18 SKIP. 신규 test-cd78-invariant.R, test-namespace-ledger.R, test-companion-parity-*.R, test-source-receipt.R, test-figure-data.R 등.
